objective: Improve LoCoMo recall from 65% to 70% through multi-dimensional retrieval

components_implemented.name: Heuristic Reranking
components_implemented.recommendation: Remove - minimal benefit for complexity cost

components_implemented.name: Policy-Guided Graph Traversal
components_implemented.recommendation: Make conditional - adds noise in session-based retrieval

components_implemented.name: Multi-turn Aggregation
components_implemented.root_cause: Operates post-retrieval, can't fix missing evidence in top-K

longmemeval_oracle.finding: NEGATIVE TRANSFER - Sprint 17 optimizations hurt LongMemEval

key_findings: Multi-turn aggregation is the unsolved core problem (Categories 1 & 3 at 16% and 23%)
Graph traversal shows negative transfer (-0.8% on LongMemEval)
Post-retrieval fixes (reranking, aggregation) can't overcome retrieval ceiling
BM25 + RRF are solid core components that generalize well
65.2% represents a reasonable ceiling for pure retrieval on LoCoMo

keep: BM25 keyword search - clear benefit, generalizes well
make_conditional: Graph traversal - only expand when seed quality > 0.7
remove: Heuristic reranking - 0.2% gain doesn't justify complexity
future_work: Accept Categories 3 queries need QA-level reasoning (out of scope)
Consider hybrid retrieval + QA system for comprehensive solution

conclusion: Sprint 17 achieved +0.3% improvement on LoCoMo (65.2%) but fell short of 70% target. Root cause: multi-turn aggregation requires architectural changes beyond post-retrieval fixes. Recommend accepting 65% as retrieval baseline and pivoting to QA-enhanced system for remaining gap.
